questions.question: What is a boundary according to this lesson?

options.text: A wall you put up to punish people
The recognition that you are a separate person with your own needs, feelings, and limits
A way to avoid all relationships
A sign that you are being difficult

questions.explanation: A boundary is the line where you end and another person begins. It is the recognition that you are a separate person with your own needs, feelings, and limits -- not a wall or punishment.

questions.question: What are the four common types of boundaries?

options.text: Work, home, school, and social
Time, emotional, communication, and physical
Morning, afternoon, evening, and weekend
Family, friends, colleagues, and strangers

questions.explanation: The four common boundary types are: Time (protecting how you spend your time), Emotional (protecting your emotional energy), Communication (setting limits on how people talk to you), and Physical (personal space and comfort with touch).

questions.question: What are the three common fears that make boundary-setting feel scary?

options.text: Fear of failure, fear of success, and fear of change
Fear of disappointing others, fear of conflict, and fear of abandonment
Fear of the unknown, fear of judgment, and fear of rejection
Fear of embarrassment, fear of poverty, and fear of illness

questions.explanation: Setting boundaries often triggers deep fears of disappointing others (making them angry or sad), conflict (the other person arguing), and abandonment (the person leaving if you set a boundary).

questions.question: What often happens when you start setting healthy boundaries?

options.text: Everyone leaves you
Unhealthy people may resist, but healthy relationships become stronger and more authentic
Nothing changes
You become more isolated

questions.explanation: Unhealthy people may get angry and leave, but healthy, respectful people will hear you, adjust, and your relationship will become stronger and more authentic. Boundaries filter for the right people.

questions.question: Why is a lack of boundaries described as a hidden source of anxiety?

options.text: Because boundaries are required by law
Because saying 'yes' to things you do not want to do leads to exhaustion and resentment that fuels anxiety
Because people without boundaries are always alone
Because boundaries make you more productive at work

questions.explanation: When personal boundaries are crossed, you often feel exhausted after conversations and say 'yes' to things you do not want to do, leading to resentment. This cycle is a major, hidden source of anxiety.

title: The Art of the Healthy Boundary - Quiz
